meaning: A man who masturbates.
cultural_context: The most famous word in the Greek language. It can be a deadly insult yelled in traffic ('Asshole!'), or a term of endearment between close friends ('Hey dude!'). Tone is everything.
example_usage: Τι κάνεις ρε μαλάκα; (Ti kaneis re malaka? - What are you doing, dude/asshole?)

meaning: The verb to engage in sexual intercourse.
cultural_context: The root of almost all severe Greek cursing. Can be conjugated infinitely to curse anything.
example_usage: Γαμώ την πουτάνα μου. (Gamo tin poutana mou. - Fuck my whore/luck.)

meaning: An exclamation of frustration derived from gamo.
cultural_context: Extremely common. Used when you drop something or make a mistake.
example_usage: Α, γαμώτο! (Ah, gamoto! - Ah, dammit!)

meaning: Commanding someone to go have sex with themselves.
cultural_context: A direct, aggressive dismissal designed to start a fight or end an argument.
example_usage: Άντε γαμήσου ρε! (Ade gamisou re! - Go fuck yourself, man!)

meaning: A derogatory slur for a homosexual man.
cultural_context: A harsh insult targeting someone's masculinity. Can also colloquially mean a sneaky, unfair person.
example_usage: Τον πούστη! (Ton pousti! - The bastard/faggot!)

meaning: A prostitute.
cultural_context: Deeply offensive toward women. Also used abstractly to describe a terribly chaotic situation ('ta ekane poutana' - he made it a whore / ruined everything).
example_usage: Είσαι μεγάλη πουτάνα. (Eisai megali poutana. - You are a massive whore.)

meaning: Feces.
cultural_context: Used to describe bad situations or yelled when things go wrong.
example_usage: Όλα είναι σκατά. (Ola einai skata. - Everything is shit.)

meaning: A disrespectful, low-class young man.
cultural_context: Usually used by older people to insult disrespectful teenagers.
example_usage: Φύγε από δω ρε τσογλάνι! (Fige apo do re tsoglani! - Get out of here you little punk!)

meaning: The physical testes.
cultural_context: Used to call out nonsense ('You're speaking balls' = You're talking bullshit), or to describe someone annoying ('Mou espases ta arhidia' = You broke my balls).
example_usage: Μας έπρηξες τα αρχίδια! (Mas eprixes ta archidia! - You swelled our balls! / You're so annoying!)

meaning: Historically derived from 'kariola' (a bed used by prostitutes). Now means a complete bastard.
cultural_context: A very heavy insult regarding someone's character.
example_usage: Είναι μεγάλος καριόλης. (Einai megalos kariolis. - He's a massive motherfucker.)

meaning: Female genitalia.
cultural_context: Highly offensive term, both literally and when used to insult someone's courage or nature.
example_usage: Βούλωστο ρε μουνί. (Voulosto re mouni. - Shut it, you cunt.)

meaning: A rag used for menstruation. A filthy person.
cultural_context: An intensely degrading insult used primarily against men to strip them of dignity.
example_usage: Είσαι ένα σκατένιο μουνόπανο. (Eisai ena skatenio mounopano. - You are a shitty cunt-rag.)

meaning: The buttocks or anus.
cultural_context: A prefix powerhouse. Added to the front of almost anything to make it worse (kolospito = ass-house, a terrible house).
example_usage: Σκάσε, κωλόπαιδο! (Skase, kolopaido! - Shut up, ass-child/brat!)

word: γαμώ το σπίτι σου (gah-mo to spee-tee sou)
english_equivalent: fuck your entire family / fucking bastard
meaning: Violating the target's household, implying sexual violence against their family.
cultural_context: The 'house/home' is sacred in Greek culture. Attacking it verbally is a severe, escalating insult.
example_usage: Γαμώ το σπίτι σου! (Gamo to spiti sou! - [Self explanatory])

word: γαμώ την Παναγία σου (gah-mo teen pah-nah-yee-ah sou)
meaning: A direct sexual insult directed at the Holy Virgin.
cultural_context: Greece is deeply Orthodox. This is one of the most offensive blasphemies possible, guaranteeing hostility from older or religious Greeks.
example_usage: Γαμώ την Παναγία σου και τον Χριστό σου! (Fuck your Virgin Mary and your Christ!)

meaning: A foolish or deeply unintelligent person.
cultural_context: Not explicitly vulgar, but widely used.
example_usage: Είσαι εντελώς βλάκας! (Eisai entelos vlakas! - You're a complete idiot!)

meaning: A moron.
cultural_context: Slightly more formal-sounding than 'vlakas', but carries the exact same weight.
example_usage: Τι ηλίθιος που είναι. (Ti ilithios pou einai. - What an idiot he is.)

meaning: Commanding someone to go to the devil/hell.
cultural_context: A classic dismissal. Stronger than 'buzz off' but lighter than 'go fuck yourself'.
example_usage: Α, άι στο διάολο ρε! (Ah, ai sto diaolo re! - Ah, go to hell man!)

meaning: Literally the son of a prostitute.
cultural_context: Will almost always result in a physical fight. The Greek equivalent of the Spanish 'Hijo de puta'.
example_usage: Έλα δω ρε πουτάνας γιε! (Ela do re poutanas yie! - Come here you son of a bitch!)

english_equivalent: fuck off / get out of here
meaning: Derived from the Turkish 'siktir' (fuck/fuck off).
cultural_context: A severe legacy curse from the Ottoman era. Widely used by older generations.
example_usage: Άι σιχτίρ από 'δω! (Ai sihtir apo 'do! - Fuck off out of here!)

meaning: Commanding someone to consume feces.
cultural_context: Rude dismissal, used universally.
example_usage: Άντε φάε σκατά! (Ade fae skata! - Go eat shit!)

meaning: Calling someone mentally delayed.
cultural_context: Used against people acting incredibly slow or failing to understand basic concepts.
example_usage: Είσαι τέλεια καθυστέρηση. (Eisai teleia kathisterisi. - You are a total retard.)

word: γαμώ τον Χριστό σου (gah-mo ton khrees-to sou)
meaning: A direct vulgar attack on Jesus Christ connected to the person's faith.
cultural_context: Among the absolute worst things you can yell in public in Greece.
example_usage: Γαμώ τον Χριστό σου, οδηγέ! (Fuck your Christ, driver!)

word: γαμώ τον σταυρό σου (gah-mo ton stahv-ro sou)
meaning: An insult against the Holy Cross.
cultural_context: Part of the holy trinity of Greek blasphemy (Christ, Mary, Cross).
example_usage: Γαμώ τον σταυρό που σε βάφτισε! (I fuck the cross that baptized you!)

meaning: A terrible idea, a useless object, or a massive mistake.
cultural_context: The noun form of malakas. If you broke a plate, you did a 'malakia'. If you bought a useless toy, it's a 'malakia'.
example_usage: Έκανα μεγάλη μαλακία. (Ekana megali malakia. - I made a huge fucking mistake.)

meaning: Another word for testicles, slightly more slangy than arhidia.
cultural_context: Often used to mean 'nothing'. 'Pira ta paparia mou' = 'I got my balls' / I got absolutely nothing.
example_usage: Παπάρια μάντολες! (My balls! / Bullshit!)

word: πάρε μου μια πίπα (pah-reh moo meeya pee-pah)
meaning: Requesting fellatio ('pipa' means a smoking pipe, slang for oral sex).
cultural_context: Direct and offensive comeback.
example_usage: Έλα πάρε μου μια πίπα. (Come suck my dick.)

meaning: An older, brutal insult for a horrible, manipulative woman.
cultural_context: Much uglier sounding than 'bitch'.
example_usage: Σκάσε μωρή σκρόφα. (Shut up, you sow.)

meaning: An animal's snout, used to describe an ugly or untrustworthy human face.
cultural_context: A 'kolo-moutro' is an ass-face.
example_usage: Κοίτα το μούτρο του. (Look at his ugly mug.)

meaning: A portmanteau of dick (psoli) and intestine (antero).
cultural_context: Highly creative vulgarity usually aimed at tall, skinny men you hate.
example_usage: Τι θες ρε ψωλάντερο; (What do you want, you dick-intestine?)

meaning: Heavy slang for male genitalia.
cultural_context: Vulgar noun. To do something 'me tin psoli' (with the dick) means to do it incredibly poorly.
example_usage: Γράφω με την ψωλή μου! (I write with my dick! / I'm doing a terrible job!)

meaning: A woman whose ass has been ruined from too much sex.
cultural_context: Extreme slut-shaming insult.
example_usage: Είναι μια βρωμερή ξεκωλιάρα. (She is a filthy torn-ass slut.)

meaning: A common weed/vegetable in Greece. Used to mean someone with the IQ of a vegetable.
cultural_context: A fantastic, poetic, non-vulgar insult.
example_usage: Τι βλίτο που είσαι! (What a vegetable you are!)

meaning: A mildly less severe version of poutana, meaning a slutting or cheap woman.
cultural_context: Commonly yelled in dramatic TV arguments.
example_usage: Φύγε από δω μωρή τσούλα. (Get out of here, you tramp.)

meaning: Being caught in your robe outside. Means a public embarrassment.
cultural_context: 'Me ekanes rompa' (You made me a robe) means 'You humiliated me'.
example_usage: Έγινα ρόμπα! (I became a robe! / I was totally embarrassed!)

meaning: I defecated myself (with apathy).
cultural_context: The absolute standard phrase for 'I couldn't care less'.
example_usage: Χέστηκα για το τι λες. (I shit myself about what you say / I couldn't care less.)

meaning: A neuter noun derived from malakas, usually directed at annoying teenagers or animals.
cultural_context: Very commonly shouted at reckless teenage drivers or annoying kids.
example_usage: Τι κάνει αυτό το μαλακισμένο; (What is that little brat doing?)

meaning: A man who thinks men are superior due to having a penis.
cultural_context: A modern political/social insult against toxic masculinity.
example_usage: Μην είσαι τέτοιος φαλοκράτης. (Don't be such a chauvinist.)

word: πάρε τα αρχίδια μου (pah-reh tah ar-khee-dee-ah mou)
english_equivalent: suck my dick / grab my balls
meaning: Telling someone they will receive nothing but your genitals.
cultural_context: Often paired with a grab of the crotch.
example_usage: Θες λεφτά; Πάρε τα αρχίδια μου! (You want money? Take my balls!)

meaning: A terrible, disrespectful child or youth.
cultural_context: Mitzes the 'kolo-' prefix with 'paidi' (child). High-utility curse for older Greeks.
example_usage: Αυτό το κωλόπαιδο μου έσπασε το τζάμι. (That little ass-child broke my window.)

meaning: A man who has been cheated on (bearing the horns).
cultural_context: Deep Mediterranean insult targeting a man's pride.
example_usage: Ο κερατάς δεν ξέρει τίποτα. (The cuckold knows nothing.)

meaning: A medically diminutive person. Used as a targeted insult.
cultural_context: Attacking a short man's height is a classic, brutal playground insult.
example_usage: Σκάσε ρε νάνε. (Shut up, you dwarf.)

meaning: Used largely by racist elements in Greece to insult any brown-skinned immigrant or a filthy place.
cultural_context: A modern, highly sensitive slur reflecting recent immigration tensions.
example_usage: Το έκανες σαν Πακιστάν εδώ μέσα. (You made it like Pakistan in here. [Filthy])

meaning: Meaning someone's head is completely hollow and only makes noise, like a tin can.
cultural_context: A great vintage insult.
example_usage: Είναι εντελώς τενεκές. (He is a complete tin can.)

meaning: Someone who is thoroughly penetrated or completely unreliable.
cultural_context: Usually describes a snitch or someone extremely sketchy.
example_usage: Αυτός ο τύπος είναι τρύπιος. (That guy is full of holes / untrustworthy.)

meaning: A noun form for someone who fucks houses.
cultural_context: Taking 'gamo to spiti sou' and making it a job title. Incredible linguistic flexibility.
example_usage: Ο γαμωσπίτης φταίει! (The house-fucker is to blame!)

meaning: An unthinking beast.
cultural_context: Yelled at bad drivers constantly. 'Tee kaneis, re zoo?!' (What are you doing, you animal?!)
example_usage: Πρόσεχε ρε ζώο! (Watch out, you animal!)

meaning: A young cow.
cultural_context: Used to describe someone large, clumsy, and stupid.
example_usage: Κουνήσου ρε μοσχάρι. (Move, you calf.)

meaning: An owl. Culturally seen as a bird that sits around staring stupidly.
cultural_context: A classic old-world insult.
example_usage: Κοιτάει σαν μπούφος. (He's staring like an owl.)

word: γαμώ το κέρατο μου (gah-mo to keh-rah-to mou)
meaning: Cursing one's own fate/horns.
cultural_context: A bizarre but incredibly common exclamation of personal frustration.
example_usage: Γαμώ το κέρατο μου, άργησα! (Fuck my horn, I'm late!)

meaning: Someone who is blind or sight-impaired.
cultural_context: Another classic traffic insult. 'Are you blind?!'
example_usage: Στραβός είσαι; Δεν βλέπεις; (Are you blind? Can't you see?)

meaning: The physical middle finger used for flipping someone off.
cultural_context: Rather than flipping the bird, Greek speakers 'give the ass-finger'.
example_usage: Του έδειξα το κωλοδάχτυλο. (I showed him the ass-finger.)

meaning: Someone covered in soot or dirt.
cultural_context: Mostly a childish or affectionate insult now.
example_usage: Έλα δω ρε μουτζούρη. (Come here, smudged one.)

meaning: The traditional Greek curse gesture: throwing an open palm with spread fingers at someone's face.
cultural_context: Historically meant rubbing ash or feces in an enemy's face. The physical equivalent of screaming 'fae skata' (eat shit). Extremely offensive.
example_usage: Του έριξα μια μούτζα. (I threw him a moutza.)

meaning: A face made of shit.
cultural_context: Used against someone you intensely dislike looking at.
example_usage: Φύγε ρε σκατοφατσά. (Get lost, shit-face.)

meaning: A noun for someone who is a fucker.
cultural_context: Almost identical in weight to kariolis.
example_usage: Είσαι γάργαρος γαμιόλης! (You are a clear fucker!)

meaning: A taxi driver.
cultural_context: Used as an insult because Greek taxi drivers are notoriously aggressive and swear constantly.
example_usage: Οδηγείς σαν ταξιτζής! (You drive like a taxi driver!)

english_equivalent: piece of shit / son of a whore
meaning: The literal semen of a prostitute. Used as a label for a despicable person.
cultural_context: One of the most biologically vile, utterly degrading things you can call a human being in Greek.
example_usage: Ψόφα ρε πουτανόσπερμα! (Drop dead, you whore-sperm!)

word: γλύψε τα αρχίδια μου (ghleeb-seh tah ar-khee-dee-ah mou)
meaning: Commanding an enemy to lick your genitals.
cultural_context: The standard 'fuck you' rejoinder.
example_usage: Γλύψε τα αρχίδια μου ρε! (Lick my balls, man!)

word: γαμώ τον θεό σου (gah-mo ton the-o sou)
meaning: A direct sexual insult directed at God.
cultural_context: The pinnacle of Greek blasphemy. Illegal to scream in public under older laws, and highly taboo.
example_usage: Γαμώ τον θεό σου! (I fuck your God!)

meaning: A terrible, creepy, or annoying old man.
cultural_context: Applying the 'kolo' (ass) prefix to age.
example_usage: Σκάσε ρε κωλόγερε. (Shut up, ass-old-man.)

meaning: A command to seal up one's mouth.
cultural_context: Intensely aggressive and dismissive.
example_usage: Βούλωστο ρε μαλάκα. (Shut it, asshole.)

meaning: An act of betrayal or a sneaky maneuver, characteristic of a 'whore'.
cultural_context: Used when someone backstabs you or cheats in a game.
example_usage: Μου έκανε πουτανιά. (He pulled a whore-trick on me.)

meaning: Fellatio.
cultural_context: Highly vulgar. 'Paizei tsimbouki' means to give a blowjob.
example_usage: Θα μου πάρεις ένα τσιμπούκι; (Will you give me a blowjob?)

meaning: An intensified form of tsoglani.
cultural_context: Shows off the Greek ability to add augmentative suffixes (-aras) to curses.
example_usage: Είσαι μεγάλος τσογλαναράς! (You're a massive punk!)

meaning: Someone who bangs their dick. A poetic version of Malakas.
cultural_context: The imagery of 'thundering your dick' equates to useless masturbatory bragging.
example_usage: Άι στο διάολο ρε ψωλοβρόντη! (Go to hell, you dick-thunderer!)

meaning: Calling someone fat.
cultural_context: Body shaming is highly normalized in Greek insults.
example_usage: Κουνήσου ρε χοντρέ! (Move it, fatty!)

meaning: A girlfriend or attractive woman.
cultural_context: Slightly sexist/objectifying slang. 'Kologomena' (ass-babe) means a trashy girl.
example_usage: Κοίτα τι γκόμενα είναι αυτή. (Look what a babe she is.)

meaning: A liar.
cultural_context: Used aggressively in arguments.
example_usage: Είσαι μεγάλος ψεύτης! (You're a massive liar!)

meaning: Someone who has been continually fucked by life or other men.
cultural_context: Vocative case insult. Highly abusive.
example_usage: Ψόφα ρε γαμημένε! (Die, you fucked/wretched bastard!)
